title: Create a Book Model and Register in Admin

content.problemStatement:
In this partially worked out example, we'll create a simple Book model and register it in the Django Admin interface which will allow us to manage books through the admin panel.

These are the steps we followed:
1. We created a new project - `library_project` and added an app `library`
2. In the library/models.py file, we have defined a `Book` model with fields `title`, `author` and `publication_year`. 
3. In the library/admin.py file, we register the `Book` model as follows:
```python
   from django.contrib import admin
   from .models import Book

   admin.site.register(Book)
```
### Do the following:
4. Create and apply migrations:
```   
   cd library_project
   python manage.py makemigrations
   python manage.py migrate
```
5. Create a superuser (admin).
```
   python manage.py createsuperuser
```
   Provide a username, email & password for the admin. Eg. `chef` `chef@codechef.com` `chef@123`

6. Run the development server:
```
   python manage.py runserver 0.0.0.0:3000
```

7. When the browser window opens, switch to fullscreen/new tab and append `/admin` at the end of the URL which will open the admin login portal.

8. Log in with your superuser credentials that you created.

9. You should now see the "Books" section in the admin interface. Click on it to add, view, edit, or delete books.


You've now successfully created a Book model and registered it in the Django Admin interface. You can manage your books through this interface without writing additional views or templates.
